text.title: Visual Regression Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Visual Regression Testing` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every snapshot baseline MUST list a named human owner (handle + role) who approves diffs. Group / team handles are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous ownership of baselines causes drift to be merged silently; a named owner forces accountability per stylesheet change.

rule.statement: Pixel-diff threshold MUST be pinned per story (e.g. `tolerance: 0.1%`); a global threshold is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-story thresholds reflect the rendered area; a global tolerance either over-rejects animations or under-rejects layout breaks.

rule.statement: Snapshot capture MUST cover ≥ 2 engines (Chromium + WebKit). Single-engine captures are rejected.
rule.rationale: WebKit/Chromium rendering deltas on flex + grid commonly produce 1-2% diffs that single-engine pipelines never see.

rule.statement: Stories that diff > 3× in a rolling 30-day window MUST move to a `quarantine/` baseline set with explicit re-stabilisation owner + due date.
rule.rationale: Unmanaged flakes train reviewers to rubber-stamp diffs; quarantine forces a real fix instead of normalising noise.

rule.statement: Visual baselines MUST be regenerated automatically on any change under `tokens/*.json`; baseline drift without a token-change trail is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: When baselines drift without a token PR, the source-of-truth for `--color-*` and `--space-*` has diverged — a forensics flag.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
